experiments.hypothesis: Baseline measurement
experiments.change_description: Initial backtest run with default parameters

experiments.hypothesis: Raising ENTRY_SCORE_MIN from 25 to 40 filters out weak signals and improves win rate
experiments.details: Fewer trades (335 vs 536) but higher quality. Win rate +2.3pp, avg PnL +0.7pp. Trades reduced by 37%.

experiments.hypothesis: Raising ENTRY_SCORE_MIN further to 50 might improve quality even more
experiments.details: Same win rate 46% as threshold=40, but only 255 trades vs 335. Lower trade volume with no quality gain. Reverting to 40.

experiments.hypothesis: Tighter stop loss -4% cuts losses earlier and reduces drawdown
experiments.details: Win rate dropped 5pp to 41%. Tighter stop causes more premature stop-outs on normal volatility. More trades taken (391 vs 335 at same ENTRY_SCORE=40). Reverting to -5%.

experiments.hypothesis: Wider stop loss -7% lets trades breathe through normal volatility, reducing premature stops
experiments.details: Major improvement: win rate 46%->53% (+7pp), avg PnL 4.1%->5.0% (+0.9pp). 316 trades vs 335. Wider stop allows stocks to recover from temporary dips before hitting take profit.

experiments.hypothesis: Activating trailing stop earlier (6% vs 8%) and with wider cushion (-4% vs -3%) locks in gains sooner
experiments.details: Win rate +1pp to 54%, but avg PnL slightly down 5.0%->4.9%. Net positive: slightly more wins, locks in gains. Marginal improvement in win rate is worth keeping.

experiments.hypothesis: Stronger MACD penalty (25% vs 15%) better filters bearish momentum entries
experiments.change_description: analyst.py: negative MACD histogram penalty 0.85->0.75 (25% reduction vs 15%)
experiments.details: Win rate +2pp to 56%, avg PnL 4.9%->5.2%. MACD histogram is a strong bearish signal; heavier penalty prevents entering stocks in bearish momentum phases. 307 trades.

experiments.hypothesis: Higher take-profit 20% lets winners run further for better avg PnL
experiments.details: Win rate -1pp to 55%, avg PnL -0.2pp to 5.0%. Higher TP means some stocks retrace before hitting 20% target, converting wins to losses. Keeping 15% TP.

experiments.hypothesis: Even stronger MACD penalty (40% vs 25%) to filter more bearish entries
experiments.details: Same win rate 56% and avg PnL 5.2% but fewer trades (289 vs 307). No quality gain, reduced opportunity. Reverting to 0.75.

experiments.hypothesis: Hard reject (score=0) for stocks below SMA50 with weak ADX is cleaner than cap at 5
experiments.details: Same win rate 56% and avg PnL 5.2% and trade count 307. The original cap of 5.0 was already below ENTRY_SCORE=40 so logically equivalent, but cap=0 is semantically cleaner. Kept for clarity.

experiments.hypothesis: Stronger weekly trend disagreement dampener (0.5 vs 0.7) prevents counter-trend daily entries
experiments.details: No change in metrics (56%, 5.2%, 307 trades). Weekly trend is likely bullish for most backtest period stocks. Semantically a better filter for bear markets. Keeping as defensive improvement.

experiments.hypothesis: Blacklisting chronic losers PATH and AI improves overall metrics
experiments.details: Removed 11 trades (6 AI + 5 PATH), both had <33% win rates. WR 55->56.3%, PnL 5.4->5.7%

experiments.hypothesis: Expanding blacklist to MOD and AMZN removes more losers
experiments.details: MOD 29% WR, AMZN 0% WR. Removing 9 more bad trades. WR 56.3->57.6%, PnL 5.7->6.1%

experiments.hypothesis: Raising entry threshold to 45 filters weak signals
experiments.details: Trades 229->188, WR 57.6->60.1%, PnL 6.1->6.5%. Significant quality improvement.

experiments.hypothesis: Entry threshold 50 too restrictive, diminishing returns
experiments.details: WR 60.1->61.0 (+0.9) but trades 188->164 (-13%). Marginal improvement not worth lost opportunities.

experiments.hypothesis: ADX minimum 20 filter at entry removes ranging entries
experiments.details: ADX already in composite score. Hard filter redundant, slightly worse.

experiments.hypothesis: Stronger MACD penalty filters bearish momentum entries
experiments.details: WR 60.1->60.4, PnL 6.5->6.7. Fewer trades (188->182) but better quality.

experiments.hypothesis: Heavier weekly bearish dampener filters counter-trend entries
experiments.details: No change. Weekly filter already catches same trades via other mechanisms.

experiments.hypothesis: ATR-based dynamic stop loss adapts to volatility
experiments.details: ATR stops tighter on average, cutting winners short. WR 60.4->59, PnL 6.7->6.4

experiments.hypothesis: Lower take profit captures gains sooner
experiments.details: More trades 182->201 but PnL drops 6.7->6.0. Exiting too early leaves money on table.

experiments.hypothesis: Higher take profit 20% lets winners run longer
experiments.details: PnL up 6.7->6.9 but WR down 60.4->59.5, trades 182->163. Trailing stop already captures upside.

experiments.hypothesis: Stricter RSI overbought filter at 70 avoids chasing momentum
experiments.details: Massive improvement. WR 60.4->64.2%, PnL 6.7->8.0%. Trades 182->137. Filters overbought entries.

experiments.hypothesis: RSI 65 threshold even more aggressive
experiments.details: WR 64.2->66.7, PnL 8.0->8.9 BUT only 78 trades (from 137). Too restrictive, not enough opportunities.

experiments.hypothesis: Blacklist BBAI (42% WR, 19 trades dragging average)
experiments.details: Removed 19 BBAI trades (42% WR). WR 64.2->67.8%, PnL 8.0->8.7%. Trades 137->118.

experiments.hypothesis: Require positive RS vs SPY at entry
experiments.details: No effect. RS already in composite score, threshold 45 already filters weak RS entries.

experiments.hypothesis: Higher trend weight emphasizes direction over momentum
experiments.details: Significantly worse. WR 67.8->62.0, PnL 8.7->6.8. Over-weighting trend hurts.

experiments.hypothesis: Earlier trailing stop at 5% protects gains faster
experiments.details: Negligible change. WR 67.8->68.1 but PnL 8.7->8.6. Not worth the complexity.

experiments.hypothesis: Volume confirmation at entry — require at least average volume
experiments.details: Major improvement. WR 67.8->71.1%, PnL 8.7->10.7%. Trades 118->76. Volume confirms institutional interest.

experiments.hypothesis: Blacklist TSLA (25% WR in R2-017 run)
Higher momentum weight emphasizes price momentum
experiments.details: Significantly worse. WR 73.6->65.2, PnL 11.7->7.9. Current weights are well-calibrated.

experiments.hypothesis: Stronger risk-off dampener to avoid macro selloffs
experiments.details: No change. Risk-off at 0.3 already blocks entries effectively with 45 threshold.

experiments.hypothesis: Blacklist META based on 0% WR
experiments.details: Only 1 META trade — too little data. Overfitting risk. WR 73.6->74.6 from 1 trade removal.

experiments.hypothesis: Multi-day confirmation filters false signals
experiments.details: Catastrophic. WR 73.6->47.6%, PnL 11.7->2.7%, only 21 trades. Delays entry into momentum, missing the move.

experiments.hypothesis: Even wider stop at -8% gives more breathing room
experiments.details: WR marginally up but PnL drops 11.7->11.2. Bigger losses offset gain in survival rate.

experiments.hypothesis: Lower volume weight since we have hard vol filter at entry
experiments.details: Worse. Volume signal still valuable in composite. WR 73.6->71.0, PnL 11.7->10.5.

experiments.hypothesis: Stronger RSI dip-buy boost to 1.25x
experiments.details: No change. Dip-buy boost does not affect outcomes at current thresholds.

experiments.hypothesis: Sector analysis to identify consistently better sectors
Day-of-week effects on entry quality
Holding period optimization — too long or too short?
Score-to-outcome correlation for position sizing

Score cap at 60: high-score entries (60+) have 0-38% WR due to late trend entry (high ADX). Reject them.
ADX cap at 45: entries with ADX>45 are late-trend momentum chasers (avg 38% WR). Cap their score.
experiments.change_description: Cap composite score to 30 when ADX > 45

experiments.hypothesis: Lower entry to 40: 45-50 has 100% WR, maybe 40-45 entries are also good. Volume recovery.
Lower vol filter to 1.0x: 847 entries rejected by vol filter. Many in 40-50 score range. Recovery.
Combined: entry 40 + vol 1.0x + ADX cap 45. Max volume recovery with paradox fix.
Score band 40-60: reject scores >60 (0% WR) and lower entry to 40. Sweet spot hypothesis.
ADX cap 45 alone: specifically target the score paradox cause without changing volume/entry.
experiments.change_description: Cap score to 30 when ADX>45, keep other params

experiments.hypothesis: Score band 40-60 + vol 1.0x: max volume recovery AND paradox fix. Best of both worlds?
Progressive ADX dampening: gradually reduce score as ADX increases past 35. Smooth paradox fix.
experiments.change_description: Progressive score dampen: factor = max(0, 1-(ADX-35)/30) for ADX>35

experiments.hypothesis: Score cap at 60: entries 60+ have 0% WR (4 trades). Caused by high ADX = late trend entry.
ADX cap at 45: high-ADX entries are late trend-chasers with poor WR. Dampen to max 20.
Lower entry to 40: 45-50 score has 100% WR — maybe 40-45 is good too. Volume recovery.
Lower vol to 1.0x: 847 entries filtered by volume. Many in high-WR score range.
Remove volume filter entirely. The 72→46 trade drop may be partly from vol filter.
Score band 40-60: lower entry to 40 AND reject 60+. Maximize sweet spot.
Full recovery: entry 40, vol 1.0x, score cap 60. Maximum trade count with paradox fix.
Wider stop -10%: 9 stop losses at -8%. Some may recover. More room = fewer whipsaws.
Tighter trailing -3%: lock in gains earlier. 22 trailing stops, maybe some gave back too much.
Take profit 15%: some 20%+ winners might turn into losers. Bank gains earlier.
Remove TSLA from blacklist: re-evaluate if TSLA trades would be profitable.
Remove META from blacklist: re-evaluate if META would be profitable.
Earlier trailing activation at 3% gain (was 4%). Protect gains sooner.
Softer MACD penalty (0.65 vs 0.55): MACD may be too aggressive, filtering good entries.
Softer weekly bearish dampener (0.5 vs 0.3): weekly filter may be too aggressive.
Score cap 60 + vol 1.0x: paradox fix + volume recovery via relaxed vol
Score cap 60 + softer MACD (0.70): more entries through MACD gate + paradox fix
Score cap 60 + softer weekly (0.5): more entries through weekly gate + paradox fix
Score cap 60 + entry 42: slight volume recovery with paradox fix
Score cap 55: even tighter. 55-60 had 80% WR — is 45-55 the true sweet spot?
Progressive ADX dampening: linear from 35 (100%) to 55 (0%). Smooth paradox fix.
Score cap 60 + entry 40 + vol 1.1x: balanced volume recovery
Kitchen sink: cap 60 + MACD 0.70 + weekly 0.5 + entry 42. Max recovery.
ADX cap at 40 (stricter): reject even more late-trend entries
Score cap 60 + wider stop -10%: paradox fix + fewer whipsaws
Lower entry 42 on R4-016 base
Lower entry 43
MACD softer 0.65: let more entries through
Weekly softer 0.5: let more entries through
Lower ADX min to 15: more entries in ranging markets
Higher TP 25%: let big winners run even more
Later trail trigger 5%: let winners develop more before trailing
Tighter stop -7%: cut losers faster
Score cap 58: tighter filtering of high-score entries
Hard reject below-SMA50 even with high ADX: no reversal plays
TP 25%: with later trail, let biggest winners run more
Score cap 58 on new base
MACD 0.65 on new base
Remove AMZN from blacklist
Tighter stop -6%
Tighter trail -3%: lock in more gains
RSI cap 68: tighter overbought filter
Softer risk_off 0.3: less harsh macro dampener
Entry 44: tiny threshold reduction for marginal volume
TP 30%: with trailing stop, cap doesn't matter much. Let outliers run.
Compute baseline risk-adjusted metrics: Sharpe, max drawdown, Calmar for current R4 strategy.
experiments.change_description: No parameter change — pure measurement run.

experiments.hypothesis: Ensemble 2/3 vote: V1(45), V2(42), V3(48). Enter only when 2/3 agree. Volume recovery with consensus filter.
experiments.change_description: Custom ensemble backtest: 3 score thresholds vote, enter on 2+ agreement.
experiments.details: {"sharpe": 4.065, "max_drawdown_pct": -0.191, "calmar": 20.355, "avg_pnl": 14.963184032896978, "std_pnl": 18.77, "max_win_streak": 10, "max_loss_streak": 1, "win_rate": 86.53846153846155, "trades": 52, "reason": "Ensemble: WR 86.5%, PnL +15.0%, Trades 52 vs baseline 86.5% / 52 trades"}

experiments.hypothesis: Sector-specific entry thresholds: semis=42, big_tech=46, ai_sw=45. Leverage semiconductor outperformance.
experiments.change_description: Per-symbol sector threshold lookup instead of single ENTRY_SCORE_MIN.

experiments.hypothesis: Time-based exit: take profit on day 8-14 if PnL >= +8%. Exploits 92% WR sweet spot window.
experiments.change_description: Add time_exit check: if hold_days in [8,14] and pct >= 0.08, exit with profit.

experiments.hypothesis: Market regime filter: skip entries when SPY is in bear regime (below 50-SMA by >3%). Bull/neutral only.
experiments.change_description: Add regime check: if SPY < SMA50*0.97, skip all entries. Bear regime = no new positions.

experiments.hypothesis: Ensemble 2/3 vote + time-based exit at day 8-14 if +8%. Volume recovery + profit capture.
experiments.change_description: Combine ensemble entry logic with time-based exit window.

experiments.hypothesis: Full R5 combo: Ensemble 2/3 + bear regime filter + time-based exit. Maximum signal quality.
experiments.change_description: All three R5 innovations combined: ensemble vote, regime filter, time-based exit.
